Question: Considering greenhouse gas measurements from (1) the 2012 MSM-02 ombrotrophic hummock — ericaceous cover site with a labile root-exudate enriched horizon indicating rhizosphere priming located in the tidal flat conjunctive-flow zone with seasonal salt-wedge intrusion; (2) the 2025 MSM-04 palustrine pool edge with dense bryophyte carpet site with a labile root-exudate enriched horizon indicating rhizosphere priming located in the anthropogenic drainage corridor influenced by ditching and subsidence; and (3) the 2025 MSM-10 riparian floodplain bench subject to seasonal inundation site with a pyrogenic carbon enriched surficial horizon from historic burns located in the interfluvial plateau with capillary fringe recession dynamics — what is the combined total greenhouse gas flux recorded across these three specific environments?
SQL Query: SELECT SUM(GreenhouseGasFluxRate) AS GreenhouseGasFluxRate FROM (SELECT GreenhouseGasFluxRate AS GreenhouseGasFluxRate FROM PeatlandMicrositeFluxCatalogue WHERE PlotIdentifier = "MSM-02 ombrotrophic hummock - ericaceous cover" AND BiomassStoichiometryClass = "labile root-exudate enriched horizon indicating rhizosphere priming" AND HydrogeomorphicPosition = "tidal flat conjunctive-flow zone with seasonal salt-wedge intrusion" AND SamplingCampaignYear = "2012" UNION ALL SELECT GreenhouseGasFluxRate AS GreenhouseGasFluxRate FROM PeatlandMicrositeFluxCatalogue WHERE PlotIdentifier = "MSM-04 palustrine pool edge with dense bryophyte carpet" AND BiomassStoichiometryClass = "labile root-exudate enriched horizon indicating rhizosphere priming" AND HydrogeomorphicPosition = "anthropogenic drainage corridor influenced by ditching and subsidence" AND SamplingCampaignYear = "2025" UNION ALL SELECT GreenhouseGasFluxRate AS GreenhouseGasFluxRate FROM PeatlandMicrositeFluxCatalogue WHERE PlotIdentifier = "MSM-10 riparian floodplain bench subject to seasonal inundation" AND BiomassStoichiometryClass = "pyrogenic carbon enriched surficial horizon from historic burns" AND HydrogeomorphicPosition = "interfluvial plateau with capillary fringe recession dynamics" AND SamplingCampaignYear = "2025");

Question: What is the combined total yearly microbial respiration due to decomposition across these four peatland settings: the perched hollow central site experiencing periodic flooding from river backflows with iron-associated organic particle transport and slow-decaying dwarf shrub litter; the rewetted farm peatland with a persistent moisture fringed zone supporting methane-producing microbes, where organic matter seeps into intertwined stream channels from banks and woody plants are in a moderate stage of decay releasing lignin-rich detritus; and finally, at the coastal peat tongue, where a long water retention period under low evaporation feeds two different organic carbon transport pathways—one through underground fen transition water flow associated with a persistent sedge mat low in nitrogen, and the other via surface runoff through Sphagnum-based litter allowing fen sedge with irregular senescence waves?
SQL Query: SELECT SUM(AnnualHeterotrophicRespiration) AS AnnualHeterotrophicRespiration FROM (SELECT AnnualHeterotrophicRespiration AS AnnualHeterotrophicRespiration FROM PeatlandCarbonLedger WHERE PeatBlockID = "PB-Depress-05 — perched hollow central" AND HydroseralResidence = "intermittent surface inundation driven by fluvial backwater" AND LateralDOCLeachingPathway = "colloidal transport associated with iron-organic complexes" AND VegetationSenescenceClass = "litter-accumulating dwarf shrub assemblage slow decomposition" UNION ALL SELECT AnnualHeterotrophicRespiration AS AnnualHeterotrophicRespiration FROM PeatlandCarbonLedger WHERE PeatBlockID = "PB-Reclaimed-16 — rewetted agricultural peat" AND HydroseralResidence = "persistent capillary fringe sustaining methanogenic microsites" AND LateralDOCLeachingPathway = "bank seepage to adjacent anastomosing stream channels" AND VegetationSenescenceClass = "woody shrub intermediate senescence and lignin-rich fallout" UNION ALL SELECT AnnualHeterotrophicRespiration AS AnnualHeterotrophicRespiration FROM PeatlandCarbonLedger WHERE PeatBlockID = "PB-Blanket-07 — coastal peat tongue" AND HydroseralResidence = "long-tail residence under low evapotranspirative flux" AND LateralDOCLeachingPathway = "subsurface matrix flow via fen transition lenses" AND VegetationSenescenceClass = "cyperaceae mat persistent senescence with low nitrogen content" UNION ALL SELECT AnnualHeterotrophicRespiration AS AnnualHeterotrophicRespiration FROM PeatlandCarbonLedger WHERE PeatBlockID = "PB-Blanket-07 — coastal peat tongue" AND HydroseralResidence = "long-tail residence under low evapotranspirative flux" AND LateralDOCLeachingPathway = "surficial overland flow through Sphagnum-derived litter" AND VegetationSenescenceClass = "fen sedge clumping with asynchronous senescence waves");

Question: What is the combined carbon stock, measured in megagrams per hectare, found in the specific wetland areas consisting of a 2018-established mangrove fringe dominated by cordgrass on a high-tide platform with underlying peat and characterized by sulfur-rich, oxygen-deprived soil layers smelling of hydrogen sulfide; the same mangrove site but featuring salt flats with lingering cyanobacteria and mineral crusts exhibiting iron-reducing microzones near roots; plus a backbarrier zone dominated by algae mats with ephemeral flatlands transitioning to sedge fens on silty alluvial soils showing an oxygen-rich gradient near root zones; and also nearby brackish rush wetlands interlaced with rhizome peat deposits in methane-emitting reduced soil layers enriched in dissolved organic compounds?
SQL Query: SELECT SUM(ArealCarbonInventory) AS ArealCarbonInventory FROM (SELECT ArealCarbonInventory AS ArealCarbonInventory FROM BlueCarbonTransectInventory WHERE TransectFolio = "TF-WF-014 mangrove fringe chronosequence established 2018 with pneumatophores" AND MarshMorphotype = "macro-tidal cordgrass-dominated high-platform with peat veneer" AND PorewaterRedoxFacet = "anoxic sulfidic core with discrete pyritic laminae and H2S odor" UNION ALL SELECT ArealCarbonInventory AS ArealCarbonInventory FROM BlueCarbonTransectInventory WHERE TransectFolio = "TF-WF-014 mangrove fringe chronosequence established 2018 with pneumatophores" AND MarshMorphotype = "salt panne with cyanobacterial mat relicts and evaporitic crusts" AND PorewaterRedoxFacet = "iron-reducing microdomain adjacent to root exudate hotspots" UNION ALL SELECT ArealCarbonInventory AS ArealCarbonInventory FROM BlueCarbonTransectInventory WHERE TransectFolio = "TF-AL-002 algal-mat dominated backbarrier transect with ephemeral flats" AND MarshMorphotype = "sedge fen transition on alluvial silty veneer indicating freshening" AND PorewaterRedoxFacet = "oxic-sulfidic gradient proximal to rhizosphere with rapid O2 turnover" UNION ALL SELECT ArealCarbonInventory AS ArealCarbonInventory FROM BlueCarbonTransectInventory WHERE TransectFolio = "TF-AL-002 algal-mat dominated backbarrier transect with ephemeral flats" AND MarshMorphotype = "brackish rush meadow interleaved with rhizome peat laminae" AND PorewaterRedoxFacet = "methanogenic reduced horizon with dissolved organic acetate pool");
